## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH source AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
),
cleaned AS (
    SELECT
        -- Id: use source id directly, mapped to Salesforce-style format
        'OPP-' || SUBSTRING(TRIM(id) FROM 5) AS "Id",

        -- Name: trim whitespace
        TRIM(name) AS "Name",

        -- StageName: normalize all variations to enum values
        CASE
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'quali', 'qualifikation') THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('won', 'gewonnen', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'closed lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
            -- Handle German wrapped formats
            WHEN stagename ILIKE '%Gewonnen%' THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN stagename ILIKE '%Verloren%' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "StageName",

        -- CloseDate: parse multiple formats into YYYY-MM-DD
        CASE
            -- ISO format: 2027-01-15
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
            -- YYYYMMDD format: 20260401
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            -- DD.MM.YYYY format: 16.11.2026
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(REPLACE(closedate, '.', '/'), 'DD/MM/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            -- MM/DD/YYYY format: 2/6/2025 or 8/1/2025
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "CloseDate",

        -- Amount: strip currency prefixes, handle European format, "None" → NULL
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(amount) = 'None' OR TRIM(LOWER(TRIM(amount))) = 'none' THEN NULL
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
                -- European format: dots as thousand separators, comma as decimal point
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''), ',')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d+,\d+$' THEN
                -- Decimal comma without thousand separators: 1234,56
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), ',', '.'))::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d+\.\d+$' THEN
                -- Standard decimal format: 1234.56 or with thousand dots like 1.234.56
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''))::DOUBLE PRECISION
            ELSE
                CASE WHEN TRIM(amount) ~ '^\-?\d+(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
                    -- European with thousand dots and decimal comma
                    CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''), ',') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                ELSE
                    -- Try simple cast after stripping non-numeric (except - . ,)
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€$,]+', '')::DOUBLE PRECISION
                END
        END AS "Amount",

        -- CurrencyIsoCode: map various formats to ISO 4217 codes
        CASE
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro', '€') THEN 'EUR'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar', '$') THEN 'USD'
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('gbp', '£') THEN 'GBP'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",

        -- AccountId: use source accountid directly (CUST-XXXX format matches Account.Id)
        TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",

        -- Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c: populate from source id
        TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c"

    FROM source
)

SELECT
    "Id",
    "Name",
    COALESCE("StageName", 'Prospecting') AS "StageName",
    COALESCE("CloseDate", NULL) AS "CloseDate",
    "Amount",
    COALESCE("CurrencyIsoCode", 'USD') AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    "AccountId",
    "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM cleaned;
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- opportunity.id: Source opportunity id (e.g. OPP-00001). Extracted as 'OPP-' prefix + numeric suffix for Salesforce-style Id.
  - Name <- opportunity.name: Direct copy with TRIM to remove whitespace.
  - StageName <- opportunity.stagename: Normalised German/English/mixed stage names to target enum via CASE: e.g. 'Gewonnen'/'Won'/'Closed Won' → Closed Won, 'Verloren'/'Lost' → Closed Lost, 'Qualifikation'/'Quali' → Qualification, 'In Prüfung' → Needs Analysis, 'Prospect'/'In Kontakt' → Prospecting. NULL source falls back to 'Prospecting'.
  - CloseDate <- opportunity.closedate: Parsed from multiple formats (YYYY-MM-DD, YYYYMMDD, MM/DD/YYYY, DD.MM.YYYY) into ISO YYYY-MM-DD text. Returns NULL if unparseable.
  - Amount <- opportunity.amount: Cleans European format amounts (dots as thousand-sep, comma as decimal), strips currency prefixes ('EUR ', '$'), converts 'None' → NULL, then casts to DOUBLE PRECISION. Negative values preserved.
  - CurrencyIsoCode <- opportunity.currencyisocode: Maps names/symbols to ISO 4217 codes: Euro/€ → EUR, Dollar/$ → USD, chf → CHF, £ → GBP. Default fallback is 'USD'.
  - AccountId <- opportunity.accountid: Direct reference from source accountid (format CUST-XXXX). Matches Salesforce Account.Id format in target schema.
  - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c <- opportunity.id: Raw source opportunity id (e.g. OPP-00001) for row-level verification.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Not present in source data; set to NULL.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Not present in source data; set to NULL.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): No deletion flag in source; defaults to 0 (not deleted).
Reasoning: The model handles all messy aspects of the source data: multiple stagename formats (English/German/mixed) are normalised to the target enum via CASE, dates are parsed from 4+ different string formats into ISO YYYY-MM-DD using regex guards before TO_DATE, and amounts with European formatting (dots+comma) or currency prefixes are cleaned with REGEXP_REPLACE before casting. AccountId references source CUST-XXXX format directly matching Salesforce Account.Id.

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a colleague's dbt mapping proposal.

You receive the full source/target context and EVERY proposed dbt model with its column notes. Review the proposal as a whole — cross-table consistency (shared keys, duplicated logic, conflicting casts), per-table correctness against the target schema, and PostgreSQL validity.

Verify that output columns use double-quoted aliases matching the target spec exactly (mixed-case names must not be left unquoted).

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise

Only flag real problems. Do not request stylistic rewrites.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 13, 22, 54, 19, 249289, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_messy_data_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."account" LIMIT 10`.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

```sql
    CASE

        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(customer_tier__c)) IN ('silver', 'silber') THEN 'Silver'

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }}
```

Reasoning: The Account model directly maps the fixture_messy_data_v2_src.account table with key transformations: INITCAP for Name/Industry normalization, a CASE expression mapping German tier names ('Platin'→Platinum, 'SILBER'→Silver) plus case-insensitive matching to the Gold/Silver/Bronze/Platinum enum domain. The NOT NULL Name constraint is satisfied via COALESCE with 'Unknown'. Source fields not present in the staging table (CreatedDate, LastModifiedDate, IsDeleted) default to NULL/0. ## Contact
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    c."id" AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(c.firstname)) AS "FirstName",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(COALESCE(c.lastname, '')) = '' THEN 'Unknown'
        ELSE INITCAP(TRIM(c.lastname))
    END AS "LastName",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.email, ''))) IN ('', 'n/a') THEN NULL
        ELSE c.email
    END AS "Email",
    TRIM(c.phone) AS "Phone",
    INITCAP(TRIM(c.title)) AS "Title",
    CASE
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.role__c, ''))) IN ('DECISION MAKER', 'ENTSCHEIDER') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.role__c, ''))) IN ('END USER', 'ENDANWENDER') THEN 'End User'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.role__c, ''))) IN ('TECHNICAL CONTACT', 'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER', 'TECHNIKER') THEN 'Technical Contact'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.role__c, ''))) IN ('EXECUTIVE SPONSOR', 'SPONSOR') THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('de', 'deutsch', 'german') THEN 'DE'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('en', 'englisch', 'english') THEN 'EN'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('fr', 'français', 'french', 'französisch') THEN 'FR'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('es', 'español', 'spanisch', 'spanish') THEN 'ES'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('it', 'italiano', 'italian', 'italienisch') THEN 'IT'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    COALESCE(a."id", c.accountid) AS "AccountId",
    c."id" AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'contact') }} c

LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a
    ON TRIM(c.accountid) = TRIM(a."id")

```
Column notes:
  - Id <- contact.id: Direct copy of source contact id as primary key
  - FirstName <- contact.firstname: INITCAP and TRIM applied for normalization
  - LastName <- contact.lastname: INITCAP, TRIM; defaults to 'Unknown' if empty/null to satisfy NOT NULL constraint
  - Email <- contact.email: NULLs and 'N/A' values replaced with NULL; raw email preserved otherwise

  - Title <- contact.title: INITCAP and TRIM applied for normalization (German job titles cleaned)
  - Role__c <- contact.role__c: Maps German, English, and mixed values to enum: Decision Maker (Entscheider), End User (Endanwender), Technical Contact (Technischer Ansprechpartner/Techniker), Executive Sponsor (Sponsor). N/A and empty → NULL
  - Preferred_Language__c <- contact.preferred_language__c: Normalizes German (Deutsch/deutsch), English (Englisch/englisch), French (Französisch/französisch) and ISO codes to uppercase 2-letter country codes. Others → NULL
  - AccountId <- account.id, contact.accountid: LEFT JOIN contact.accountid to account.id; returns Salesforce-style Account Id (CUST-xxxx format). Fallback to raw accountid if no match.
  - Legacy_Contact_ID__c <- contact.id: Natural key for row-level verification

Reasoning: The Contact model maps source columns with case-insensitive enum normalization for Role__c (German/English variants like Entscheider→Decision Maker, Sponsor→Executive Sponsor) and Preferred_Language__c (Deutsch→DE, French→FR, etc.). AccountId is resolved via LEFT JOIN to the account table on accountid = account.id to return the Salesforce-style Account Id. ## Installed_Asset__c
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    -- Primary key (mapped directly from source)
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",

    -- Name: NOT NULL, use name with sensible default
    COALESCE(TRIM(name), 'Unknown Asset') AS "Name",

    -- Serial number
    serial_number__c AS "Serial_Number__c",

    -- Warranty end date: parse multiple formats, return ISO YYYY-MM-DD or NULL
    CASE
        WHEN warranty_end_date__c IS NULL
             OR warranty_end_date__c = 'N/A'
             OR warranty_end_date__c = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
        -- ISO format: 2029-05-02
        WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$'
            THEN TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
        -- Compact numeric: 20291103
        WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$'
            THEN TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        -- European dot-separated: 27.02.2030
        WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{4}$'
            THEN TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        -- US slash-separated: 7/9/2030 or 12/20/2028
        WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$'
            THEN TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Warranty_End_Date__c",

    -- Account reference: source account__c already uses Salesforce-style id format (CUST-XXXX)
    CAST(account__c AS TEXT) AS "Account__c",

    -- Project reference: source project__c already uses Salesforce-style id format (PROJ-XXXXX)
    CAST(project__c AS TEXT) AS "Project__c",

    -- Legacy asset ID from source natural key
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Legacy_Asset_ID__c",

    -- Derived / synthetic fields
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "CreatedDate",
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'installed_asset__c') }}
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- id: Source primary key, passed through as Salesforce-style asset Id
  - Name <- name: Asset name; uses COALESCE with 'Unknown Asset' to satisfy NOT NULL constraint
  - Serial_Number__c <- serial_number__c: Source serial number field passed through directly
  - Warranty_End_Date__c <- warranty_end_date__c: Multi-format date parsing: ISO (YYYY-MM-DD), compact (YYYYMMDD), European dot (DD.MM.YYYY), US slash (MM/DD/YYYY). Invalid/sentinel values ('0000-00-00', 'N/A') mapped to NULL
  - Account__c <- account__c: Source account reference already in Salesforce Account Id format (CUST-XXXX), passed through directly to link to Account.Id
  - Project__c <- project__c: Source project reference already in Salesforce Project__c Id format (PROJ-XXXXX), passed through directly
  - Legacy_Asset_ID__c <- id: Source natural key stored for row-level verification and lineage tracing
  - CreatedDate <- (none): No source value available; set to NULL as no creation timestamp exists in the staging data
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): No source value available; set to NULL as no modification timestamp exists in the staging data
  - IsDeleted <- (none): No deletion flag in source; defaulting to 0 (not deleted) as there is no source indicator
Reasoning: The Installed_Asset__c model maps directly from the source installed_asset__c table with straightforward column pass-throughs for Id, Name, Serial_Number__c, Account__c, Project__c, and Legacy_Asset_ID__c. The key transformation is multi-format date parsing on warranty_end_date__c (ISO, compact YYYYMMDD, European DD.MM.YYYY, US MM/DD/YYYY) with invalid sentinel values mapped to NULL. Account__c and Project__c reference fields already match Salesforce-style Id formats in the source so direct pass-through is appropriate. CreatedDate/LastModifiedDate are NULL since no timestamps exist in source; IsDeleted defaults to 0.

## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH source AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
),
cleaned AS (
    SELECT
        -- Id: use source id directly, mapped to Salesforce-style format
        'OPP-' || SUBSTRING(TRIM(id) FROM 5) AS "Id",

        -- Name: trim whitespace
        TRIM(name) AS "Name",

        -- StageName: normalize all variations to enum values
        CASE
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'quali', 'qualifikation') THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('won', 'gewonnen', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'closed lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
            -- Handle German wrapped formats
            WHEN stagename ILIKE '%Gewonnen%' THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN stagename ILIKE '%Verloren%' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "StageName",

        -- CloseDate: parse multiple formats into YYYY-MM-DD
        CASE
            -- ISO format: 2027-01-15
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
            -- YYYYMMDD format: 20260401
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            -- DD.MM.YYYY format: 16.11.2026
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(REPLACE(closedate, '.', '/'), 'DD/MM/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            -- MM/DD/YYYY format: 2/6/2025 or 8/1/2025
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "CloseDate",

        -- Amount: strip currency prefixes, handle European format, "None" → NULL
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(amount) = 'None' OR TRIM(LOWER(TRIM(amount))) = 'none' THEN NULL
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
                -- European format: dots as thousand separators, comma as decimal point
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''), ',')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d+,\d+$' THEN
                -- Decimal comma without thousand separators: 1234,56
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), ',', '.'))::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d+\.\d+$' THEN
                -- Standard decimal format: 1234.56 or with thousand dots like 1.234.56
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''))::DOUBLE PRECISION
            ELSE
                CASE WHEN TRIM(amount) ~ '^\-?\d+(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
                    -- European with thousand dots and decimal comma
                    CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''), ',') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                ELSE
                    -- Try simple cast after stripping non-numeric (except - . ,)
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€$,]+', '')::DOUBLE PRECISION
                END
        END AS "Amount",

        -- CurrencyIsoCode: map various formats to ISO 4217 codes
        CASE
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro', '€') THEN 'EUR'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar', '$') THEN 'USD'
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('gbp', '£') THEN 'GBP'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",

        -- AccountId: use source accountid directly (CUST-XXXX format matches Account.Id)
        TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",

        -- Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c: populate from source id
        TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c"

    FROM source
)

SELECT
    "Id",
    "Name",
    COALESCE("StageName", 'Prospecting') AS "StageName",
    COALESCE("CloseDate", NULL) AS "CloseDate",
    "Amount",
    COALESCE("CurrencyIsoCode", 'USD') AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    "AccountId",
    "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM cleaned;
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- opportunity.id: Source opportunity id (e.g. OPP-00001). Extracted as 'OPP-' prefix + numeric suffix for Salesforce-style Id.
  - Name <- opportunity.name: Direct copy with TRIM to remove whitespace.
  - StageName <- opportunity.stagename: Normalised German/English/mixed stage names to target enum via CASE: e.g. 'Gewonnen'/'Won'/'Closed Won' → Closed Won, 'Verloren'/'Lost' → Closed Lost, 'Qualifikation'/'Quali' → Qualification, 'In Prüfung' → Needs Analysis, 'Prospect'/'In Kontakt' → Prospecting. NULL source falls back to 'Prospecting'.
  - CloseDate <- opportunity.closedate: Parsed from multiple formats (YYYY-MM-DD, YYYYMMDD, MM/DD/YYYY, DD.MM.YYYY) into ISO YYYY-MM-DD text. Returns NULL if unparseable.
  - Amount <- opportunity.amount: Cleans European format amounts (dots as thousand-sep, comma as decimal), strips currency prefixes ('EUR ', '$'), converts 'None' → NULL, then casts to DOUBLE PRECISION. Negative values preserved.
  - CurrencyIsoCode <- opportunity.currencyisocode: Maps names/symbols to ISO 4217 codes: Euro/€ → EUR, Dollar/$ → USD, chf → CHF, £ → GBP. Default fallback is 'USD'.
  - AccountId <- opportunity.accountid: Direct reference from source accountid (format CUST-XXXX). Matches Salesforce Account.Id format in target schema.
  - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c <- opportunity.id: Raw source opportunity id (e.g. OPP-00001) for row-level verification.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Not present in source data; set to NULL.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Not present in source data; set to NULL.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): No deletion flag in source; defaults to 0 (not deleted).
Reasoning: The model handles all messy aspects of the source data: multiple stagename formats (English/German/mixed) are normalised to the target enum via CASE, dates are parsed from 4+ different string formats into ISO YYYY-MM-DD using regex guards before TO_DATE, and amounts with European formatting (dots+comma) or currency prefixes are cleaned with REGEXP_REPLACE before casting. AccountId references source CUST-XXXX format directly matching Salesforce Account.Id.

```sql
        WHEN 'planung' THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN 'on hold' THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN 'pausiert' THEN 'On Hold'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE
        -- Skip obvious invalid dates
        WHEN go_live_date__c IS NULL OR TRIM(go_live_date__c) = '' OR LOWER(TRIM(go_live_date__c)) = 'n/a' OR go_live_date__c = '0000-00-00' THEN NULL
        -- YYYY-MM-DD format (ISO, 10 chars)
        WHEN LENGTH(TRIM(go_live_date__c)) = 10 AND TRIM(go_live_date__c) ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(TRIM(go_live_date__c), 'YYYY-MM-DD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        -- YYYYMMDD format (8 digits)

        -- MM/DD/YYYY format (US, variable length but contains '/')
        WHEN TRIM(go_live_date__c) ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(TRIM(go_live_date__c), 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        -- DD.MM.YYYY format (European, contains '.')
        WHEN TRIM(go_live_date__c) ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(TRIM(go_live_date__c), 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    CAST(account__c AS TEXT) AS "Account__c",
    CAST(opportunity__c AS TEXT) AS "Opportunity__c",

    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'project__c') }}
```

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

The `dbt_sql` field must contain executable PostgreSQL SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or commentary outside SQL string literals.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 13, 23, 0, 51, 242010, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='A reviewer rejected your dbt model for `Contact`.

Previous SQL:
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    c."id" AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(c.firstname)) AS "FirstName",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(COALESCE(c.lastname, '')) = '' THEN 'Unknown'
        ELSE INITCAP(TRIM(c.lastname))
    END AS "LastName",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.email, ''))) IN ('', 'n/a') THEN NULL
        ELSE c.email
    END AS "Email",
    TRIM(c.phone) AS "Phone",
    INITCAP(TRIM(c.title)) AS "Title",
    CASE
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.role__c, ''))) IN ('DECISION MAKER', 'ENTSCHEIDER') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.role__c, ''))) IN ('END USER', 'ENDANWENDER') THEN 'End User'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.role__c, ''))) IN ('TECHNICAL CONTACT', 'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER', 'TECHNIKER') THEN 'Technical Contact'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.role__c, ''))) IN ('EXECUTIVE SPONSOR', 'SPONSOR') THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('de', 'deutsch', 'german') THEN 'DE'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('en', 'englisch', 'english') THEN 'EN'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('fr', 'français', 'french', 'französisch') THEN 'FR'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('es', 'español', 'spanisch', 'spanish') THEN 'ES'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('it', 'italiano', 'italian', 'italienisch') THEN 'IT'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    COALESCE(a."id", c.accountid) AS "AccountId",
```

- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text

So the fixes needed:
1. `CAST(NULL AS TEXT)` for CreatedDate and LastModifiedDate
2. For AccountId, instead of `COALESCE(a."id", c.accountid)`, we should do something safer. The reviewer says "Use a consistent key transform or COALESCE to a safe default/NULL instead of raw keys." So I'll change it to just use `a."id"` (which will be NULL if the join fails) - this is safer.
3. Remove quotes around lowercase source column names like `"id"`, `firstname`, etc.

Actually, wait - looking more carefully at point 3: "Consider removing `."id"` quotes around source columns in the SELECT list as they are lowercase by definition; unquoted is standard." This means changing things like `c."id"` to just `c.id` since PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase anyway.

Let me write the corrected SQL:

```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    c.id AS Id,
    INITCAP(TRIM(c.firstname)) AS FirstName,
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(COALESCE(c.lastname, '')) = '' THEN 'Unknown'
        ELSE INITCAP(TRIM(c.lastname))

    CASE

    CASE
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.role__c, ''))) IN ('DECISION MAKER', 'ENTSCHEIDER') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.role__c, ''))) IN ('END USER', 'ENDANWENDER') THEN 'End User'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.role__c, ''))) IN ('TECHNICAL CONTACT', 'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER', 'TECHNIKER') THEN 'Technical Contact'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.role__c, ''))) IN ('EXECUTIVE SPONSOR', 'SPONSOR') THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS Role__c,
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('de', 'deutsch', 'german') THEN 'DE'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('en', 'englisch', 'english') THEN 'EN'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('fr', 'français', 'french', 'französisch') THEN 'FR'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('es', 'español', 'spanisch', 'spanish') THEN 'ES'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(c.preferred_language__c, ''))) IN ('it', 'italiano', 'italian', 'italienisch') THEN 'IT'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS Preferred_Language__c,
    a.id AS AccountId,
    c.id AS Legacy_Contact_ID__c,
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS CreatedDate,
    CAST(NULL AS TEXT) AS LastModifiedDate,
    0 AS IsDeleted

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'contact') }} c

LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }} a
    ON TRIM(c.accountid) = TRIM(a.id)
```

Wait, let me check the original again - it used `COALESCE(a."id", c.accountid)` and now we change to just `a.id`. But looking at the target column type for AccountId: `AccountId` text. Let me also make sure about `a.id` vs `a."id"` - since `a.` is a table alias and we're accessing its `id` column, unquoted should be fine for lowercase identifiers.

Actually, I notice the reviewer comment 3 specifically mentions removing quotes from source columns like `"id"`. For the join condition and the AccountId column, using `a.id` instead of `a."id"` is correct.

Let me also check if there are any other issues to address while we're at it - the previous SQL looks mostly good otherwise.

One thing I want to double-check: the reviewer said "remove `."id"` quotes around source columns in the SELECT list as they are lowercase by definition; unquoted is standard." This means changing `c."id"` to `c.id`, etc. Let me verify all the source column references...

In the original:
- `c."id"` → change to `c.id`
- `a."id"` (in join condition) → change to `a.id`

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

The `dbt_sql` field must contain executable PostgreSQL SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or commentary outside SQL string literals.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 13, 23, 2, 1, 317755, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='A reviewer rejected your dbt model for `Opportunity`.

Previous SQL:
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH source AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
),
cleaned AS (
    SELECT
        -- Id: use source id directly, mapped to Salesforce-style format
        'OPP-' || SUBSTRING(TRIM(id) FROM 5) AS "Id",

        -- Name: trim whitespace
        TRIM(name) AS "Name",

        -- StageName: normalize all variations to enum values
        CASE
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'quali', 'qualifikation') THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('won', 'gewonnen', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'closed lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
            -- Handle German wrapped formats
            WHEN stagename ILIKE '%Gewonnen%' THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN stagename ILIKE '%Verloren%' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "StageName",

        -- CloseDate: parse multiple formats into YYYY-MM-DD
        CASE
            -- ISO format: 2027-01-15
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
            -- YYYYMMDD format: 20260401
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            -- DD.MM.YYYY format: 16.11.2026
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(REPLACE(closedate, '.', '/'), 'DD/MM/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            -- MM/DD/YYYY format: 2/6/2025 or 8/1/2025
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "CloseDate",

        -- Amount: strip currency prefixes, handle European format, "None" → NULL
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(amount) = 'None' OR TRIM(LOWER(TRIM(amount))) = 'none' THEN NULL
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
                -- European format: dots as thousand separators, comma as decimal point
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''), ',')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d+,\d+$' THEN
                -- Decimal comma without thousand separators: 1234,56
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), ',', '.'))::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d+\.\d+$' THEN
                -- Standard decimal format: 1234.56 or with thousand dots like 1.234.56
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''))::DOUBLE PRECISION
            ELSE
                CASE WHEN TRIM(amount) ~ '^\-?\d+(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
                    -- European with thousand dots and decimal comma
                    CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''), ',') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                ELSE
                    -- Try simple cast after stripping non-numeric (except - . ,)
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€$,]+', '')::DOUBLE PRECISION
                END
        END AS "Amount",

        -- CurrencyIsoCode: map various formats to ISO 4217 codes
        CASE
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro', '€') THEN 'EUR'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar', '$') THEN 'USD'
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('gbp', '£') THEN 'GBP'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",

        -- AccountId: use source accountid directly (CUST-XXXX format matches Account.Id)
        TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",

        -- Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c: populate from source id
        TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c"

    FROM source
)

SELECT
    "Id",
    "Name",
    COALESCE("StageName", 'Prospecting') AS "StageName",
    COALESCE("CloseDate", NULL) AS "CloseDate",
    "Amount",
    COALESCE("CurrencyIsoCode", 'USD') AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    "AccountId",
    "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM cleaned;
```

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

The `dbt_sql` field must contain executable PostgreSQL SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or commentary outside SQL string literals.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 13, 23, 2, 1, 317755, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='A reviewer rejected your dbt model for `Opportunity`.

Previous SQL:
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH source AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
),
cleaned AS (
    SELECT
        -- Id: use source id directly, mapped to Salesforce-style format
        'OPP-' || SUBSTRING(TRIM(id) FROM 5) AS "Id",

        -- Name: trim whitespace
        TRIM(name) AS "Name",

        -- StageName: normalize all variations to enum values
        CASE
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'quali', 'qualifikation') THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('won', 'gewonnen', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'closed lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
            -- Handle German wrapped formats
            WHEN stagename ILIKE '%Gewonnen%' THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN stagename ILIKE '%Verloren%' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "StageName",

        -- CloseDate: parse multiple formats into YYYY-MM-DD
        CASE
            -- ISO format: 2027-01-15
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
            -- YYYYMMDD format: 20260401
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            -- DD.MM.YYYY format: 16.11.2026
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(REPLACE(closedate, '.', '/'), 'DD/MM/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            -- MM/DD/YYYY format: 2/6/2025 or 8/1/2025
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "CloseDate",

        -- Amount: strip currency prefixes, handle European format, "None" → NULL
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(amount) = 'None' OR TRIM(LOWER(TRIM(amount))) = 'none' THEN NULL
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
                -- European format: dots as thousand separators, comma as decimal point
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''), ',')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d+,\d+$' THEN
                -- Decimal comma without thousand separators: 1234,56
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), ',', '.'))::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d+\.\d+$' THEN
                -- Standard decimal format: 1234.56 or with thousand dots like 1.234.56
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''))::DOUBLE PRECISION
            ELSE
                CASE WHEN TRIM(amount) ~ '^\-?\d+(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
                    -- European with thousand dots and decimal comma
                    CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''), ',') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                ELSE
                    -- Try simple cast after stripping non-numeric (except - . ,)
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€$,]+', '')::DOUBLE PRECISION
                END
        END AS "Amount",

        -- CurrencyIsoCode: map various formats to ISO 4217 codes
        CASE
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro', '€') THEN 'EUR'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar', '$') THEN 'USD'
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('gbp', '£') THEN 'GBP'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",

        -- AccountId: use source accountid directly (CUST-XXXX format matches Account.Id)
        TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",

        -- Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c: populate from source id
        TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c"

    FROM source
)

SELECT
    "Id",
    "Name",
    COALESCE("StageName", 'Prospecting') AS "StageName",
    COALESCE("CloseDate", NULL) AS "CloseDate",
    "Amount",
    COALESCE("CurrencyIsoCode", 'USD') AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    "AccountId",
    "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM cleaned;
```

Reviewer comments:
Needs revision for critical PostgreSQL syntax errors in the Amount cleaning logic: 1) `(REGEXP_REPLACE(..., ''), ',')::DOUBLE PRECISION` creates a row constructor/tuple `('string', '')` rather than concatenating or replacing strings. It should be `REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ',')::DOUBLE PRECISION`. 2) `CAST(..., ') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)` contains mismatched parentheses and incorrect CAST syntax (CAST only takes two arguments). Rewrite using properly nested REPLACE/REGEXP_REPLACE followed by a single `::DOUBLE PRECISION` cast. Additionally, `Opportunity.Id` applies `'OPP-' || SUBSTRING(...)` transformation while `Project__c.Opportunity__c` passes the raw source key through. This will break cross-table joins if source formats differ; align both to use identical transforms or explicit mapping.

# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_messy_data_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."account" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_messy_data_v2_src.account  (126 rows)

- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text

- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce a corrected dbt model for `Opportunity`. ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content="The reviewer pointed out critical issues:

1. **Amount cleaning logic has syntax errors**: The expression `(REGEXP_REPLACE(..., ''), ',')::DOUBLE PRECISION` is wrong - it creates a tuple/row constructor instead of doing the replacement. It should be `REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ',')::DOUBLE PRECISION`.

2. **CAST syntax error**: `CAST(..., ') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)` has mismatched parentheses and incorrect CAST syntax. Should use `::DOUBLE PRECISION` instead.

3. **Cross-table key alignment**: The previous model applies `'OPP-' || SUBSTRING(...)` to create the Id, but the reviewer notes that `Project__c.Opportunity__c` passes the raw source key through. This will break cross-table joins. I need to check how `opportunity.id` and `project__c.opportunity__c` relate and ensure consistent transforms.

Let me first inspect the data to understand:
1. What formats the opportunity id takes
2. How project__c.opportunity__c references opportunities
3. What the actual amount values look like

Let me query the source tables to understand the data.", id='reasoning_content', provider_name='openai'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='query_postgres', args='{"sql": "SELECT * FROM \"fixture_messy_data_v2_src\".\"opportunity\" LIMIT 15"}', tool_call_id='call_mhsvoey1'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='query_postgres', args='{"sql": "SELECT * FROM \"fixture_messy_data_v2_src\".\"project__c\" LIMIT 10"}', tool_call_id='call_awev5ryq'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='query_postgres', args='{"sql": "SELECT * FROM \"fixture_messy_data_v2_src\".\"account\" LIMIT 10"}', tool_call_id='call_v7qs8ubp')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=4646, output_tokens=393), model_name='ise-ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 13, 23, 2, 20, 293265, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 13, 23, 2, 20, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-5c681667-8fb8-4044-9ece-d1ca57bcc1ba', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f5db7-4b64-7317-8566-a09234efe473', conversation_id='019f5db7-4b64-7317-8566-a0913a80ba3b')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

The `dbt_sql` field must contain executable PostgreSQL SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or commentary outside SQL string literals.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 13, 23, 2, 1, 317755, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='A reviewer rejected your dbt model for `Opportunity`.

Previous SQL:
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH source AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
),
cleaned AS (
    SELECT
        -- Id: use source id directly, mapped to Salesforce-style format
        'OPP-' || SUBSTRING(TRIM(id) FROM 5) AS "Id",

        -- Name: trim whitespace
        TRIM(name) AS "Name",

        -- StageName: normalize all variations to enum values
        CASE
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'quali', 'qualifikation') THEN 'Qualification'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('won', 'gewonnen', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'closed lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
            -- Handle German wrapped formats
            WHEN stagename ILIKE '%Gewonnen%' THEN 'Closed Won'
            WHEN stagename ILIKE '%Verloren%' THEN 'Closed Lost'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "StageName",

        -- CloseDate: parse multiple formats into YYYY-MM-DD
        CASE
            -- ISO format: 2027-01-15
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
            -- YYYYMMDD format: 20260401
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            -- DD.MM.YYYY format: 16.11.2026
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(REPLACE(closedate, '.', '/'), 'DD/MM/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            -- MM/DD/YYYY format: 2/6/2025 or 8/1/2025
            WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "CloseDate",

        -- Amount: strip currency prefixes, handle European format, "None" → NULL
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(amount) = 'None' OR TRIM(LOWER(TRIM(amount))) = 'none' THEN NULL
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
                -- European format: dots as thousand separators, comma as decimal point
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''), ',')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d+,\d+$' THEN
                -- Decimal comma without thousand separators: 1234,56
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), ',', '.'))::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[^\d.\-,]', '', 'g') ~ '^\-?\d+\.\d+$' THEN
                -- Standard decimal format: 1234.56 or with thousand dots like 1.234.56
                (REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''))::DOUBLE PRECISION
            ELSE
                CASE WHEN TRIM(amount) ~ '^\-?\d+(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
                    -- European with thousand dots and decimal comma
                    CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ''), ',') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                ELSE
                    -- Try simple cast after stripping non-numeric (except - . ,)
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€$,]+', '')::DOUBLE PRECISION
                END
        END AS "Amount",

        -- CurrencyIsoCode: map various formats to ISO 4217 codes
        CASE
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro', '€') THEN 'EUR'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar', '$') THEN 'USD'
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
            WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('gbp', '£') THEN 'GBP'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",

        -- AccountId: use source accountid directly (CUST-XXXX format matches Account.Id)
        TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",

        -- Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c: populate from source id
        TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c"

    FROM source
)

SELECT
    "Id",
    "Name",
    COALESCE("StageName", 'Prospecting') AS "StageName",
    COALESCE("CloseDate", NULL) AS "CloseDate",
    "Amount",
    COALESCE("CurrencyIsoCode", 'USD') AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    "AccountId",
    "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM cleaned;
```

It should be `REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', ''), '\.', ',')::DOUBLE PRECISION`. 2) `CAST(..., ') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)` contains mismatched parentheses and incorrect CAST syntax (CAST only takes two arguments). Rewrite using properly nested REPLACE/REGEXP_REPLACE followed by a single `::DOUBLE PRECISION` cast.     CASE

```sql
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'won', 'gewonnen') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    
    CASE
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(REPLACE(closedate, '.', '/'), 'DD/MM/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(LOWER(TRIM(amount))) IN ('none', 'null', '') THEN NULL
        WHEN TRIM(amount) ~ '^\-?\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})+,\d+$' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(

                    ',', '.'

        ELSE
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '[A-Za-z\s€,]+', '') AS DOUBLE PRECISION
            )
    END AS "Amount",
    
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('eur', 'euro', '€') THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('usd', 'dollar', '$') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) = 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('gbp', '£') THEN 'GBP'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    
    TRIM(accountid) AS "AccountId",
    
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }};
```
